tEn: Do as I Do: Dexterous Manipulation Data from Everyday Human Videos
tZh: 照我做：从日常人类视频中获取灵巧操作数据
sEn: DO AS I DO reconstructs and retargets monocular RGB human videos to dexterous robotic hands, enabling scalable data generation for manipulation.
sZh: DO AS I DO 算法从单目 RGB 人类视频中重建并重定向灵巧机器人手部动作，实现可扩展的操作数据生成。
absEn: How can we scalably generate data for robotic manipulation, especially on human-like platforms such as dexterous multi-fingered hands? Learning from human videos has recently emerged as a likely answer to this question. However, difficulties in estimating hand-object interaction and crossing the human-to-robot embodiment gap have hindered the adoption of abundant monocular RGB-only human videos as the primary source of robot manipulation data. In this work, we present DO AS I DO, an algorithm to reconstruct and retarget monocular RGB human videos to multi-fingered dexterous robotic hands. DO AS I DO reconstructs hand-object interactions from various egocentric and exocentric in-the-wild video sources. The algorithm then retargets these hand-object interaction estimates into a sequence of actions executable in the real world, yielding robot-complete manipulation data from disparate human videos. Overall, DO AS I DO outperforms previous state of the art in estimating hand-object interactions and extracting dexterous manipulation trajectories from RGB videos, as we show in experiments on datasets with ground truths and on a dataset of video clips collected online. Our experiments enable us to propose an efficacy playbook for practitioners collecting human data for manipulation.
absZh: 如何可扩展地生成机器人操作数据，尤其是在灵巧多指手等类人平台上？从人类视频中学习近来成为这一问题的可能答案。然而，估计手-物体交互以及跨越人类到机器人的具身差距的困难，阻碍了将丰富的单目 RGB 人类视频作为机器人操作数据主要来源的采用。在这项工作中，我们提出了 DO AS I DO，一种从单目 RGB 人类视频重建并重定向到多指灵巧机器人手的算法。DO AS I DO 从各种自我中心和非自我中心的野外视频源中重建手-物体交互。然后，该算法将这些手-物体交互估计重定向为可在现实世界中执行的动作序列，从不同的人类视频中产生机器人完整的操作数据。总体而言，DO AS I DO 在估计手-物体交互和从 RGB 视频中提取灵巧操作轨迹方面优于先前的最先进技术，我们在具有真实值的数据集以及在线收集的视频片段数据集上的实验证明了这一点。我们的实验使我们能够为收集人类操作数据的实践者提出一个效能指南。

contrib.en: Introduces Do as I Do, a two-step algorithm to reconstruct and retarget behaviors from monocular RGB videos to multi-fingered dexterous hands.
contrib.zh: 提出 Do as I Do，一种从单目 RGB 视频重建并重定向行为到多指灵巧手的两步算法。

contrib.en: Hand-object reconstruction outperforms SOTA on relevant metrics and handles diverse video sources.
contrib.zh: 手-物体重建在相关指标上优于现有技术，并处理多样化的视频源。

contrib.en: Retargeting improves upon existing scalable dynamics-aware techniques with novel components for noisy references.
contrib.zh: 重定向通过新颖组件改进了现有可扩展动力学感知技术，适用于噪声参考。

contrib.en: First pipeline to go from internet video to real dexterous hand rollouts.
contrib.zh: 首个从互联网视频到真实灵巧手部署的完整流程。

contrib.en: Proposes an efficacy playbook for practitioners collecting human data for manipulation.
contrib.zh: 为从业者收集人类操作数据提出有效性指南。

contrib.en: Achieves 71% success rate on in-the-wild reconstructed data, significantly improving over baseline 25%.
contrib.zh: 在野外重建数据上达到 71%成功率，显著优于基线 25%。

limits.en: Assumes rigid objects and semi-accurate metric depth from monocular RGB.
limits.zh: 假设刚体物体和来自单目 RGB 的半准确度量深度。

limits.en: Monocular ambiguity in hand-object distance, unable to distinguish contact from occlusion.
limits.zh: 单目在手-物体距离上存在歧义，无法区分接触与遮挡。

limits.en: Reconstructs only hand and object, not full scene, limiting reasoning about environmental constraints.
limits.zh: 仅重建手和物体，而非完整场景，限制了对环境约束的推理。

limits.en: Physics simulators model real-world dynamics only approximately, bounding real-world performance.
limits.zh: 物理模拟器仅近似建模真实世界动力学，限制了真实世界性能的上限。

limits.en: Only 4% of internet videos pass quality check, indicating high data attrition.
limits.zh: 仅 4%的互联网视频通过质量检查，表明数据损耗高。

items.en: How can we scalably generate data for robotic manipulation, especially on human-like platforms such as dexterous multi-fingered hands? Learning from human videos has recently emerged as a likely answer to this question. However, difficulties in estimating hand-object interaction and crossing the human-to-robot embodiment gap have hindered the adoption of abundant monocular RGB-only human videos as the primary source of robot manipulation data. In this work, we present Do as I Do, an algorithm to reconstruct and retarget monocular RGB human videos to multi-fingered dexterous robotic hands. Do as I Do reconstructs hand-object interactions from various egocentric and exocentric in-the-wild video sources. The algorithm then retargets these hand-object interaction estimates into a sequence of actions executable in the real world, yielding robot-complete manipulation data from disparate human videos. Overall, Do as I Do outperforms previous state of the art in estimating hand-object interactions and extracting dexterous manipulation trajectories from RGB videos, as we show in experiments on datasets with ground truths and on a dataset of video clips collected online. Our experiments enable us to propose an efficacy playbook for practitioners collecting human data for manipulation.
items.zh: 我们如何可扩展地为机器人操作生成数据，尤其是在灵巧多指手等类人平台上？从人类视频中学习最近成为这个问题的可能答案。然而，手-物体交互估计的困难以及跨越人-机器人具身鸿沟的挑战阻碍了将丰富的单目 RGB 人类视频作为机器人操作数据主要来源的采用。在这项工作中，我们提出了“Do as I Do”，一种将单目 RGB 人类视频重建和重定向到多指灵巧机器人手的算法。“Do as I Do”从各种自我中心和外部中心的野外视频源中重建手-物体交互。然后，该算法将这些手-物体交互估计重定向为一系列可在现实世界中执行的动作，从不同的人类视频中生成机器人完整的操作数据。总体而言，在具有真实标签的数据集和在线收集的视频片段数据集上的实验表明，“Do as I Do”在估计手-物体交互和从 RGB 视频中提取灵巧操作轨迹方面优于先前的最先进技术。我们的实验使我们能够为从业者收集人类操作数据提出一个有效性指南。

items.capEn: Figure 1: We introduce Do as I Do, an algorithm that takes in-the-wild monocular RGB videos of hand-object interaction (top) and generates dexterous hand manipulation data (bottom).
items.capZh: 图 1：我们介绍“Do as I Do”，一种算法，它接收野外单目 RGB 手-物体交互视频（顶部）并生成灵巧手操作数据（底部）。

items.en: Data is a critical component of any learning algorithm, including robot learning. For a novice intelligent actor, be it a child, an apprentice, or a robot, however, available data for any task is mostly observational: experienced not via doing but via watching experts do it. This gap in watching and doing is easily closed by children [1, 2], but presents an insurmountable barrier for today’s robots, which rely primarily on experiential data collected via real-world teleoperation or simulated exploration. Consider the challenges of generating experiential data for dexterous behaviors as shown in Fig. 1. Teleoperation is bottlenecked by operator expertise, cost of operation, and mechanical transparency of the teleoperation rig. Exploration in simulation is similarly bottlenecked by complexities in designing diverse environments and reward functions. The natural question we ask in this work is how to close this gap by converting the accessible, observational data from humans into experiential data for robots.
items.zh: 数据是任何学习算法（包括机器人学习）的关键组成部分。然而，对于一个新手智能体，无论是儿童、学徒还是机器人，任何任务的可获得数据大多是观察性的：不是通过做，而是通过观看专家做来体验。这种观看与做之间的差距很容易被儿童弥合[1, 2]，但对于当今的机器人来说却是一个不可逾越的障碍，它们主要依赖于通过现实世界遥操作或模拟探索收集的经验数据。考虑为如图 1 所示的灵巧行为生成经验数据的挑战。遥操作受限于操作员专业知识、操作成本和遥操作装置的机械透明度。模拟中的探索同样受限于设计多样化环境和奖励函数的复杂性。我们在这项工作中提出的自然问题是如何通过将人类可获得的观察数据转换为机器人的经验数据来弥合这一差距。

items.en: Investigations into inferring robot actions from watching humans, also known as “Do as I do”, are almost as old as the field of artificial intelligence, seen notably in the 1970 MIT “copy-demo” [3], “robot instruction” by direct human demonstration [4] and “Do as I do” with action synthesis and retargeting [5]. The two primary algorithmic challenges are recognition or reconstruction of the human behavior, and retargeting of this behavior on a (possibly vastly different) robotic embodiment. Assumptions on the behaviors, i.e. pick-and-place only, and on the objects, i.e. 3D-scanned only, have made inroads into this challenge possible [6, 7]. Over time, further assumptions on the data modality, such as availability of depth, 3D, or hand keypoints in data collected with specialized hardware, have made more advances in “Do as I do” possible [8, 9]. However, most of our observational data today is stored as monocular RGB videos of humans, and therefore algorithms that can address this general case stand to deliver the largest increase in robotic data.
items.zh: 从观察人类推断机器人动作的研究，也称为“Do as I Do”，几乎与人工智能领域一样古老，显著见于 1970 年麻省理工学院的“copy-demo”[3]、通过直接人类演示的“机器人指令”[4]以及带有动作合成和重定向的“Do as I Do”[5]。两个主要的算法挑战是人类行为的识别或重建，以及将该行为重定向到（可能非常不同的）机器人具身上。对行为（例如仅抓取和放置）和物体（例如仅 3D 扫描）的假设使得这一挑战取得了进展[6, 7]。随着时间的推移，对数据模态的进一步假设，例如在专用硬件收集的数据中深度、3D 或手部关键点的可用性，使得“Do as I Do”取得了更多进展[8, 9]。然而，今天我们的观察数据大多以单目 RGB 人类视频的形式存储，因此能够处理这种一般情况的算法有望带来机器人数据的最大增长。

items.en: Here, two recent advances in adjacent fields provide us with a novel approach to this problem. First, 3D computer vision models today can take 2D RGB images and reconstruct depth [10], objects [11], and hands [12] in 3D from purely monocular RGB videos, enabling 4D hand-object estimation. Simultaneously, GPU-parallel physical simulators such as Mujoco Warp [13] and Isaac [14] enable fast sampling-based optimization algorithms [15] that we can use to infer robotic dexterous hand actions in minutes from 4D hand-object states. Note that both of these approaches make minimal assumptions on the problem structure, such as the observed behavior or target objects. Inspired by such advances, in this work we present Do as I Do, aiming to bridge this gap between observational and experiential data for dexterous manipulation. Do as I Do recognizes and retargets dexterous human actions from everyday monocular RGB videos and produces multi-fingered robotic hand and arm actions performing the same task on the environment. More importantly, we are able to do so without making limiting assumptions on the displayed behavior (e.g. no grasping priors) or object classes, supporting arbitrary rigid bodies. Concretely, the contributions of this work are as follows:
items.zh: 在这里，相邻领域的两个最新进展为我们提供了解决这个问题的新方法。首先，今天的 3D 计算机视觉模型可以从 2D RGB 图像中重建深度[10]、物体[11]和手部[12]的 3D 信息，仅从单目 RGB 视频实现 4D 手-物体估计。同时，GPU 并行物理模拟器如 Mujoco Warp[13]和 Isaac[14]支持快速基于采样的优化算法[15]，我们可以用它们从 4D 手-物体状态中在几分钟内推断出机器人灵巧手动作。请注意，这两种方法都对问题结构（例如观察到的行为或目标物体）做出了最小假设。受这些进展的启发，在这项工作中，我们提出了“Do as I Do”，旨在弥合灵巧操作中观察数据与经验数据之间的差距。“Do as I Do”从日常单目 RGB 视频中识别和重定向灵巧的人类动作，并生成多指机器人手和手臂动作，在环境中执行相同的任务。更重要的是，我们能够做到这一点，而不对显示的行为（例如无抓取先验）或物体类别做出限制性假设，支持任意刚体。具体来说，这项工作的贡献如下：

items.en: We introduce Do as I Do, a two-step algorithm to reconstruct and retarget behaviors from monocular RGB videos to multi-fingered dexterous hands.
items.zh: 我们介绍了“Do as I Do”，一种两步算法，用于从单目 RGB 视频重建和重定向行为到多指灵巧手。

items.en: Our hand-object reconstruction process outperforms SOTA on relevant metrics and handles diverse videos — ego- or exo-centric, ranging from in-the-wild internet clips to outputs of generative video models.
items.zh: 我们的手-物体重建过程在相关指标上优于 SOTA，并处理多样化的视频——自我中心或外部中心，从野外互联网片段到生成式视频模型的输出。

items.en: Our retargeting process improves upon existing scalable dynamics-aware retargeting techniques by introducing novel components that robustify the noisy reconstructed reference trajectories.
items.zh: 我们的重定向过程通过引入新颖的组件来增强对噪声重建参考轨迹的鲁棒性，改进了现有的可扩展动力学感知重定向技术。

items.en: Our robot data is playable on a dexterous robot hand and arm, completing, to the best of our knowledge, the first pipeline that can go from an internet video to real dexterous hand rollouts.
items.zh: 我们的机器人数据可在灵巧机器人手和手臂上执行，据我们所知，完成了第一个可以从互联网视频到真实灵巧手滚动的流水线。

items.en: Dexterous Manipulation from Human Videos. Many past works explore ways to leverage semantics and motions from human videos. Some extract priors via pretraining, either as visual representations [26, 27, 28], dexterous policies [29, 30, 31, 32, 33], or forward dynamics models [34, 35]. Other approaches compute more structured priors, such as affordances [36, 37], flows [38, 39], and 3D reconstructions for retargeting [6, 40, 41, 42, 16, 7, 22, 18]. Our work falls in the final category and pushes the frontier in utilizing diverse in-the-wild data sources, as shown in Table 1.
items.zh: 从人类视频进行灵巧操作。许多过去的工作探索了利用人类视频中的语义和运动的方法。一些通过预训练提取先验，无论是作为视觉表示[26, 27, 28]、灵巧策略[29, 30, 31, 32, 33]还是前向动力学模型[34, 35]。其他方法计算更结构化的先验，如可供性[36, 37]、光流[38, 39]和用于重定向的 3D 重建[6, 40, 41, 42, 16, 7, 22, 18]。我们的工作属于最后一类，并在利用多样化的野外数据源方面推动了前沿，如表 1 所示。

items.en: Hand-Object Reconstruction. Reconstructing 4D hand-object interaction from monocular RGB video decomposes along three complementary axes: hand pose estimation, object shape and pose estimation, and their joint modeling. The structured shape and motion of the human hand [43] has enabled hand tracking models [12, 44, 45] robust to motion blur, occlusion, and low resolution. Object reconstruction and tracking under the same in-the-wild noisy conditions remain substantially harder because of diversity in everyday objects. Recent progress has come from (1) image-conditioned 3D generative foundation models [46, 23, 11] with robust priors over shape and pose, which can handle occlusions and lower resolution, and (2) model-based 6-DoF trackers [17, 47] that estimate object pose given a known or jointly reconstructed mesh. However, these methods were largely validated on clean lab videos and struggle on in-the-wild noisy videos, as we show below. Finally, joint hand-object reconstruction methods reason about both hand and object signals simultaneously, and have shown progress on lab data [48] as well as in-the-wild videos [49, 50, 51]. Recent video methods remain narrow in scope: some are egocentric-only [52], while category-conditioned approaches [53, 54] assume closed object taxonomies. In contrast to these joint approaches, Do as I Do adopts a modular decomposition: HaWoR for hand tracking, SAM3D for single-image object meshing, and our SAM3D-based tracking (Sec. 3.1) for object pose evolution.
items.zh: 手-物体重建。从单目 RGB 视频重建 4D 手-物体交互沿着三个互补的轴分解：手部姿态估计、物体形状和姿态估计以及它们的联合建模。人类手部的结构化形状和运动[43]使得手部跟踪模型[12, 44, 45]对运动模糊、遮挡和低分辨率具有鲁棒性。在相同的野外噪声条件下，由于日常物体的多样性，物体重建和跟踪仍然困难得多。最近的进展来自（1）图像条件 3D 生成基础模型[46, 23, 11]，具有对形状和姿态的鲁棒先验，可以处理遮挡和较低分辨率，以及（2）基于模型的 6 自由度跟踪器[17, 47]，在已知或联合重建的网格上估计物体姿态。然而，这些方法主要在干净的实验室视频上验证，并在野外噪声视频上表现不佳，如下所示。最后，联合手-物体重建方法同时推理手和物体信号，并在实验室数据[48]和野外视频[49, 50, 51]上取得了进展。最近的视频方法仍然范围狭窄：有些仅限自我中心[52]，而类别条件方法[53, 54]假设封闭的物体分类。与这些联合方法相比，“Do as I Do”采用模块化分解：HaWoR 用于手部跟踪，SAM3D 用于单图像物体网格生成，以及我们基于 SAM3D 的跟踪（第 3.1 节）用于物体姿态演化。

items.en: Human-to-Robot Retargeting. Dexterous retargeting algorithms map human hand poses onto robot embodiments with vastly different geometries (e.g., finger link lengths and articulations). Kinematic retargeting approaches do so by solving geometric, task-space, or joint-space optimizations [55, 56, 57, 58], but operate solely on the robot configuration and do not account for forces between hand and object, often causing penetration, fingertip sliding, and grasp instability. To address this, dynamics-aware retargeting frameworks optimize physically-simulated hand-object trajectories, generally via reinforcement learning to track a reference-based reward [59, 60, 16, 61] or sampling-based optimization [62, 63, 15]. However, a key assumption shared by most prior retargeting approaches is availability of clean references with ground-truth hand-object poses from, e.g., MoCap. In contrast, we tackle the more difficult setting of noisier reconstructed references, with potential temporal discontinuities and severe hand-object misalignments.
items.zh: 人-机器人重定向。灵巧重定向算法将人类手部姿态映射到具有截然不同几何形状（例如手指连杆长度和关节）的机器人具身上。运动学重定向方法通过解决几何、任务空间或关节空间优化来实现[55, 56, 57, 58]，但仅作用于机器人配置，不考虑手与物体之间的力，常常导致穿透、指尖滑动和抓取不稳定。为了解决这个问题，动力学感知重定向框架优化物理模拟的手-物体轨迹，通常通过强化学习来跟踪基于参考的奖励[59, 60, 16, 61]或基于采样的优化[62, 63, 15]。然而，大多数先前重定向方法的一个关键假设是存在来自例如动作捕捉的具有真实手-物体姿态的干净参考。相比之下，我们处理更困难的设置，即噪声更大的重建参考，具有潜在的时间不连续性和严重的手-物体错位。

items.capEn: Figure 2: Method Overview. Our method leverages vision foundation models to reconstruct the hand and object, and retargets them onto the robot via sampling-based optimization in simulation.
items.capZh: 图 2：方法概述。我们的方法利用视觉基础模型重建手和物体，并通过模拟中的基于采样的优化将其重定向到机器人上。

items.en: Do as I Do consists of two parts, shown in Fig. 2. First, we reconstruct the 3D hand and object, and track them through time (Section 3.1). Then, we retarget the reconstructions onto the robot embodiment, producing dynamically-feasible trajectories that are effective in the real world (Section 3.2).
items.zh: “Do as I Do”由两部分组成，如图 2 所示。首先，我们重建 3D 手和物体，并随时间跟踪它们（第 3.1 节）。然后，我们将重建重定向到机器人具身上，生成在现实世界中有效的动力学可行轨迹（第 3.2 节）。

items.en: Recognizing and reconstructing hand-object interactions from in-the-wild videos can be decomposed into two components: (1) tracking the human hand, and (2) determining object shape and tracking its pose. Critically, these capabilities need to be robust to diverse visual conditions found in noisy internet videos. We find that existing models such as HawoR [45] satisfy this criterion and can directly be used for our hand tracking with reasonable performance. For open-ended rigid objects with hand occlusions, however, prior works [17, 47] tend to lose pose lock, drift, or fail to re-acquire the object once visual evidence degrades as shown in 5. Therefore, we develop our own tracking method based on 3D generative foundation models trained with occlusions, namely SAM 3D [11]. Once tracked, the 3D hand, object, and camera are composed into a consistent near metric-space. We run three processing steps: (a) hand and object segmentation using SAM 3 [64], (b) depth and camera intrinsics estimation with MoGe [10], and (c) object 3D mesh generation using SAM 3D [11].
items.zh: 从野外视频中识别和重建手-物体交互可以分解为两个组件：（1）跟踪人类手部，以及（2）确定物体形状并跟踪其姿态。关键的是，这些能力需要对嘈杂互联网视频中多样的视觉条件具有鲁棒性。我们发现现有模型如 HaWoR[45]满足这一标准，可以直接用于我们的手部跟踪，性能合理。然而，对于具有手部遮挡的开放刚体，先前的工作[17, 47]往往失去姿态锁定、漂移或在视觉证据退化时无法重新获取物体，如图 5 所示。因此，我们开发了自己的基于 3D 生成基础模型的跟踪方法，该模型经过遮挡训练，即 SAM 3D[11]。一旦跟踪完成，3D 手、物体和相机被组合成一个一致的近度量空间。我们运行三个处理步骤：（a）使用 SAM 3[64]进行手和物体分割，（b）使用 MoGe[10]估计深度和相机内参，以及（c）使用 SAM 3D[11]生成物体 3D 网格。

items.en: Object Tracking via Guided Diffusion. We repurpose SAM 3D [11], an image-to-3D generative model robust to occlusions and low resolution, into a video object tracker. It learns the joint distribution over shape and pose pθ​(xs,xp∣c)p_{\theta}(x^{s},x^{p}\mid c) given a single 2D image and object mask. As a result, it produces a different mesh each frame and a pose sequence with no temporal coherence if applied independently on frames. Our key observation is that shape and pose share the same latent space in the learned joint distribution; we can therefore fix the shape and obtain an updated pose entirely at inference time for each frame. Specifically, we fix the shape x¯s\bar{x}^{s} at an anchor frame and, given the pose xk−1px^{p}_{k-1} from the previous frame, predict the pose xkpx^{p}_{k} at frame kk. Tracking thus reduces to drawing from pθ(xkp∣xks=x¯s,ck)p_{\theta}(x^{p}_{k}\mid x^{s}_{k}=\bar{x}^{s},\,c_{k}) biased toward xk−1px^{p}_{k-1}. Marginalizing over all possible poses is infeasible in the 66-DoF continuous pose space with the large generative backbone of SAM 3D. We instead exploit the flow matching inference itself: a sample is produced by integrating the ODE x˙=vθ​(xt,t,c)\dot{x}=v_{\theta}(x_{t},t,c) from x0∼𝒩⁡(0,𝐈)x_{0}\!\sim\!\mathcal{N}(0,\mathbf{I}) along the linear path xt=(1−t)​x0+t​x1x_{t}=(1-t)\,x_{0}+t\,x_{1}. In a flow model, the forward-noised target is simply its interpolant along the model’s own probability path [65, 66]. At each Euler step, we take the model’s free Euler update of each block and blend it toward target interpolants, nudging towards canonical shape x¯s\bar{x}^{s} for the shape block, and previous-frame pose xk−1px^{p}_{k-1} for the pose block:
items.zh: 通过引导扩散进行物体跟踪。我们将 SAM 3D[11]（一种对遮挡和低分辨率鲁棒的图像到 3D 生成模型）重新用作视频物体跟踪器。它学习给定单个 2D 图像和物体掩码的形状和姿态的联合分布 pθ(xs, xp | c)。因此，如果独立应用于帧，它会为每一帧生成不同的网格和没有时间一致性的姿态序列。我们的关键观察是，形状和姿态在学习的联合分布中共享相同的潜在空间；因此，我们可以在推理时完全固定形状并为每一帧获得更新的姿态。具体来说，我们在锚定帧固定形状 x̄s，并给定前一帧的姿态 x_{k-1}^p，预测帧 k 的姿态 x_k^p。因此，跟踪简化为从 pθ(x_k^p | x_k^s = x̄s, c_k)中采样，偏向于 x_{k-1}^p。在具有 SAM 3D 大型生成骨干的 66 自由度连续姿态空间中，对所有可能的姿态进行边缘化是不可行的。我们转而利用流匹配推理本身：通过从 x0 ~ N(0, I)沿线性路径 xt = (1-t)x0 + t x1 积分 ODE ẋ = vθ(xt, t, c)来生成样本。在流模型中，前向噪声目标只是其沿模型自身概率路径的插值[65, 66]。在每个欧拉步骤中，我们取模型的每个块的自由欧拉更新，并将其向目标插值混合，对于形状块推向规范形状 x̄s，对于姿态块推向上一帧姿态 x_{k-1}^p：

items.en: where αs,αp∈[0,1]{\color[rgb]{0.1211,0.3711,0.668}\alpha_{s},\alpha_{p}}\in[0,1] are guidance strength parameters; zrefs​(t)=(1−t)​ϵs+t​x¯s{\color[rgb]{0.8516,0.4766,0.1094}z^{s}_{\mathrm{ref}}(t)}=(1-t)\,\epsilon^{s}+t\,\bar{x}^{s} and zrefp​(t)=(1−t)​ϵp+t​xk−1p{\color[rgb]{0.8516,0.4766,0.1094}z^{p}_{\mathrm{ref}}(t)}=(1-t)\,\epsilon^{p}+t\,x^{p}_{k-1} are target interpolants; ϵs,ϵp\epsilon^{s},\epsilon^{p} are the blocks’ initial noise and vθs,vθpv^{s}_{\theta},v^{p}_{\theta} are the shape and pose components of the velocity vθ​(xt−Δ,t−Δ,c)v_{\theta}(x_{t-\Delta},t-\Delta,c).
items.zh: 其中αs, αp ∈ [0,1]是引导强度参数；z_ref^s(t) = (1-t)ε^s + t x̄s 和 z_ref^p(t) = (1-t)ε^p + t x_{k-1}^p 是目标插值；ε^s, ε^p 是块的初始噪声，vθ^s, vθ^p 是速度 vθ(x_{t-Δ}, t-Δ, c)的形状和姿态分量。

items.en: Adaptive Guidance Parameters. As we focus on rigid objects, any fixed shape guidance αs∈[0.9,1]{\color[rgb]{0.1211,0.3711,0.668}\alpha_{s}}\in[0.9,1] works well. Instead of defining a fixed pose guidance αp{\color[rgb]{0.1211,0.3711,0.668}\alpha_{p}}, which may cause over-rigidity or spurious flips, we derive it from the data using rotational velocity of the object estimated from 2D point tracks [67]. This adds one offline tracking pass per video but noticeably improves pose tracking as shown in Appendix.
items.zh: 自适应引导参数。由于我们关注刚体，任何固定的形状引导αs ∈ [0.9,1]都效果良好。我们不定义固定的姿态引导αp（这可能导致过度刚性或虚假翻转），而是从数据中推导它，使用从 2D 点跟踪估计的物体旋转速度[67]。这为每个视频增加了一次离线跟踪过程，但显著改善了姿态跟踪，如附录所示。

items.en: Sampling Per-frame Poses. As explained above, the guided pose sampling in Eq. 1 is stochastic. So, at each frame kk, our algorithm draws NN candidates {xk,ip}i=1N\{x^{p}_{k,i}\}_{i=1}^{N} that share the fixed shape x¯s\bar{x}^{s} and needs to pick one of the samples per frame. The principled choice is to rank candidates by the model’s own conditional log-density over poses given a shape. However, this process takes orders of magnitude more compute over generation itself and becomes prohibitive at video scale. We hence sample and cluster poses under a weighted SE⁡(3)\mathrm{SE}(3) distance. Empirically, confident samples concentrate on the same pose mode while estimator noise scatters across SE⁡(3)\mathrm{SE}(3), so consensus filtering and mask-IoU recovers the mode-best pose without ever re-invoking the diffusion backbone.
items.zh: 每帧姿态采样。如上所述，方程 1 中的引导姿态采样是随机的。因此，在每一帧 k，我们的算法抽取 N 个候选{x_{k,i}^p}_{i=1}^N，它们共享固定形状 x̄s，并且需要每帧选择一个样本。原则性的选择是根据模型自身的给定形状下姿态的条件对数密度对候选进行排序。然而，这个过程比生成本身多花费几个数量级的计算，在视频规模下变得不可行。因此，我们在加权 SE(3)距离下对姿态进行采样和聚类。经验上，自信的样本集中在相同的姿态模式上，而估计器噪声分散在 SE(3)上，因此共识过滤和掩码 IoU 恢复了模式最佳姿态，而无需重新调用扩散骨干。

items.en: Hand-Object Alignment. After we independently reconustruct hand and object at possibly different scales, we need to align them. We treat the hand reconstruction scale as ground-truth and scale the object translation ss to be aligned with the hand. We compute hand and object centroids: 𝐜handM\mathbf{c}^{\mathrm{M}}_{\mathrm{hand}}, 𝐜objM\mathbf{c}^{\mathrm{M}}_{\mathrm{obj}} in object scale, and centroid of the visible portion of the hand mesh 𝐜handH\mathbf{c}^{\mathrm{H}}_{\mathrm{hand}} in hand mesh scale (near metric). Now, given the scaling ratio from the centroid zz values, k=zhandH/zhandMk=z^{\mathrm{H}}_{\mathrm{hand}}/z^{\mathrm{M}}_{\mathrm{hand}}, we optimize for the target object position 𝐨𝐛𝐣target=𝐜handH+k⁡(𝐜objM−𝐜handM)\mathbf{obj}_{\mathrm{target}}=\mathbf{c}^{\mathrm{H}}_{\mathrm{hand}}+k\,\bigl(\mathbf{c}^{\mathrm{M}}_{\mathrm{obj}}-\mathbf{c}^{\mathrm{M}}_{\mathrm{hand}}\bigr), where the per-frame translation scale is solved by least squares. Finally, we align the trajectory with gravity using GeoCalib [68].
items.zh: 手-物体对齐。在我们独立重建可能不同尺度的手和物体之后，我们需要对齐它们。我们将手部重建尺度视为真实值，并缩放物体平移 s 以与手部对齐。我们计算手部和物体质心：物体尺度下的 c_hand^M, c_obj^M，以及手部网格尺度（近度量）下手部网格可见部分的质心 c_hand^H。现在，给定质心 z 值的缩放比 k = z_hand^H / z_hand^M，我们优化目标物体位置 obj_target = c_hand^H + k (c_obj^M - c_hand^M)，其中每帧平移尺度通过最小二乘法求解。最后，我们使用 GeoCalib[68]将轨迹与重力对齐。

items.capEn: Figure 3: Verbs and Objects. We visualize 20 distinct actions from our pipeline: placing, picking, scrubbing, spreading, squeezing, ironing, painting, dusting, digging, erasing, pouring, writing, whisking, stirring, poking, tamping, drilling, hammering, cutting, and basting.
items.capZh: 图 3：动词与物体。我们可视化了流程中的 20 种不同动作：放置、拾取、擦洗、涂抹、挤压、熨烫、绘画、除尘、挖掘、擦除、倾倒、书写、搅拌、搅动、戳、夯实、钻孔、锤击、切割和涂油。

items.en: Next, we aim to reproduce the reconstructed hand-object trajectory on a robot hand. However, this reference is incomplete: human and robot morphologies differ, and contact information and forces are absent from the kinematic signal. Prior works address this with kinematic solvers [22] or robotic heuristics [18, 7], but they do not ensure physical plausibility or lose general-purpose expressiveness.
items.zh: 接下来，我们旨在在机器人手上复现重建的手-物体轨迹。然而，这一参考是不完整的：人类与机器人形态不同，且运动学信号中缺少接触信息和力。先前的工作通过运动学求解器 [22] 或机器人启发式方法 [18, 7] 解决此问题，但它们不能确保物理合理性或丧失了通用表达能力。

items.en: Do as I Do instead performs dynamics-aware retargeting, which follows the reference while ensuring realism within physics simulation. Building on the framework from Pan et al. 2026, we perform an MPPI-style sampling-based optimization with a kernel annealed across both iterations and the prediction horizon, which shifts from broad exploration to local refinement. To enable retargeting for noisy reconstructed references, we further highlight several novel innovations, as shown in Fig. 4.
items.zh: 相反，“照我做”执行了动力学感知的重定向，它在遵循参考的同时确保物理模拟中的真实性。基于 Pan 等人 2026 的框架，我们执行了一种 MPPI 风格的基于采样的优化，其核在迭代和预测视界上均进行退火，从广泛探索转向局部细化。为了实现对噪声重建参考的重定向，我们进一步提出了几项新颖的创新，如图 4 所示。

items.en: Warmup Steps. Two issues lie in the initial trajectory horizon HH: (1) a noisy first frame may initialize the hand and object in a state that’s impossible to recover from (e.g., if object is not grasped), and (2) annealed sampling does not fully explore these HH steps since they appear only at the start of the rollout horizon. Thus, we introduce additional HH warmup steps prepended to the reference. During warmup, the object is held in place (e.g., in mid-air) while the robot hand is free to move; afterwards, the weld is dropped and simulation proceeds as normal. This allows the robot to adjust its pose before tracking the reference (e.g., to avoid dropping the object in Fig. 4), and naturally guides the optimizer in maximizing its tracking objective. Crucially, this warmup design does not assume any grasp sampling or heuristics, and simply utilizes the pre-existing core optimization procedure.
items.zh: 预热步骤。初始轨迹视界 H 存在两个问题：(1) 噪声的第一帧可能将手和物体初始化为无法恢复的状态（例如，如果物体未被抓取），(2) 退火采样未能充分探索这些 H 步，因为它们仅出现在展开视界的开始。因此，我们引入了额外的 H 个预热步骤，附加在参考之前。在预热期间，物体被固定（例如，在半空中），而机器人手可以自由移动；之后，焊点被释放，模拟正常进行。这使得机器人能够在跟踪参考之前调整其姿态（例如，避免在图 4 中掉落物体），并自然地引导优化器最大化其跟踪目标。关键的是，这种预热设计不假设任何抓取采样或启发式方法，仅利用预先存在的核心优化过程。

items.en: Random Force Perturbation. Second, the rollout horizon may trap optimization in local minima, with unstable object interactions that track briefly but cannot recover (e.g., balancing on fingertips in Fig. 4). To address this, we assert that interactions should be robust against minor disturbances: drawing inspiration from sim-to-real [69, 70], we introduce random forces to sample rollouts, thus encouraging controls robust to such perturbations. Importantly, this solution is general-purpose and does not assume high-fidelity references, unlike alternatives (e.g., contact guidance [15]).
items.zh: 随机力扰动。其次，展开视界可能将优化困在局部最小值中，导致不稳定的物体交互，这些交互短暂跟踪但无法恢复（例如，在图 4 中指尖上的平衡）。为了解决这个问题，我们断言交互应对微小扰动具有鲁棒性：受 sim-to-real [69, 70] 的启发，我们引入随机力来采样展开，从而鼓励对此类扰动鲁棒的控制。重要的是，这种解决方案是通用的，不假设高保真参考，与替代方案（例如，接触引导 [15]）不同。

items.en: Transition Reward. Third, object transitions between “rest” and “in-hand” mark critical inflection points in the trajectory, but with noisy references, tracking reward alone is too imprecise and soft to encourage the step-function interaction induced by these transitions (e.g., failed pickup in Fig. 4). Thus, we add a constant penalty term for failed transitions: (1) lack of object-floor contact during resting reference timesteps and (2) lack of hand-object contact during in-hand reference timesteps. We define reference timestep stages by measuring reference hand-object distance under threshold ϵ\epsilon.
items.zh: 过渡奖励。第三，物体在“静止”和“手中”状态之间的过渡标志着轨迹中的关键转折点，但使用噪声参考时，仅靠跟踪奖励过于不精确和柔和，无法鼓励这些过渡所引发的阶跃函数交互（例如，图 4 中失败的拾取）。因此，我们为失败的过渡添加了一个恒定的惩罚项：(1) 在静止参考时间步中缺乏物体-地面接触，(2) 在手中参考时间步中缺乏手-物体接触。我们通过测量参考手-物体距离在阈值 ε 以下来定义参考时间步阶段。

items.capEn: Figure 4: Retargeting. Our method succeeds in common failure modes (top) and excels at handling noisy references (bottom), despite, e.g., incorrect depth estimation causing poor alignment.
items.capZh: 图 4：重定向。我们的方法成功处理了常见失败模式（顶部），并擅长处理噪声参考（底部），尽管存在例如深度估计错误导致对齐不良的情况。

items.en: We evaluate each step of our framework on standard benchmarks for their respective tasks. First, for hand-object reconstruction, we follow the setup from prior work [51, 53, 54]: we evaluate on DexYCB [71] and HOI4D [72] datasets with 160 and 12 annotated videos respectively, and isolate object-level performance by supplying ground-truth hands and measuring object reconstruction and tracking quality. We compare against two groups of baselines: (1) joint hand-object reconstructions including both image-based [48, 49, 50] and video-based [51, 54] approaches, and (2) object trackers [17, 47], where we replace our object tracking approach with these baselines keeping every other component fixed in our pipeline. To additionally assess performance on a distribution closer to everyday videos, we collect a benchmark of 150 videos drawn from in-the-wild internet videos, egocentric datasets, and generated videos. Since ground-truth object poses are unavailable for these videos, we evaluate via human preference, asking 3 volunteers per video to compare object poses from our SAM 3D-based tracking method against those from the current state of the art. Details of each of the baselines and human evaluation setup have been provided in the Appendix.
items.zh: 我们在各自任务的标准基准上评估了我们框架的每一步。首先，对于手-物体重建，我们遵循先前工作的设置 [51, 53, 54]：我们在 DexYCB [71] 和 HOI4D [72] 数据集上评估，分别有 160 和 12 个带注释的视频，并通过提供真实手部并测量物体重建和跟踪质量来隔离物体级性能。我们与两组基线进行比较：(1) 联合手-物体重建，包括基于图像 [48, 49, 50] 和基于视频 [51, 54] 的方法，以及 (2) 物体跟踪器 [17, 47]，其中我们用这些基线替换我们的物体跟踪方法，同时保持流程中所有其他组件不变。为了进一步评估更接近日常视频分布的性能，我们收集了一个包含 150 个视频的基准，这些视频来自野外互联网视频、自我中心数据集和生成视频。由于这些视频没有真实物体姿态，我们通过人类偏好进行评估，每个视频请 3 名志愿者比较我们基于 SAM 3D 的跟踪方法与当前最先进方法得到的物体姿态。每个基线和人类评估设置的详细信息已在附录中提供。

items.en: Finally, for retargeting, we evaluate on our in-the-wild reconstruction dataset of 655 reconstructed references, as well as OakInk2 [73], a large MoCap dataset with 1,352 clean bimanual human-object task trajectories. We compare against SPIDER [15], the state-of-the-art for dexterous retargeting and the only prior method designed for this scale; SPIDER serves as the Annealed Sampling baseline, and we progressively introduce our three components to assess their contributions. Following the recent literature [15, 59, 60], we evaluate successful trajectories as those with mean position error Epos<0.1E_{\text{pos}}<0.1 m and mean rotation error Erot<0.5E_{\text{rot}}<0.5 rad. Technical details are in the Appendix.
items.zh: 最后，对于重定向，我们在包含 655 个重建参考的野外重建数据集以及 OakInk2 [73]（一个包含 1,352 个干净双手人类-物体任务轨迹的大型 MoCap 数据集）上进行评估。我们与 SPIDER [15] 进行比较，后者是灵巧重定向的最先进方法，也是唯一为此规模设计的先前方法；SPIDER 作为退火采样基线，我们逐步引入三个组件以评估其贡献。遵循近期文献 [15, 59, 60]，我们将成功轨迹评估为平均位置误差 E_pos < 0.1 米且平均旋转误差 E_rot < 0.5 弧度的轨迹。技术细节见附录。

items.en: Across all tasks, we use the 22-DoF Sharpa Wave hand. Real-world deployment results shown here are on a bimanual setup with Sharpa Wave hands and UR3e arms, both commanded at 50 Hz.
items.zh: 在所有任务中，我们使用 22 自由度 Sharpa Wave 手。此处展示的真实世界部署结果是在双手设置上，使用 Sharpa Wave 手和 UR3e 手臂，均以 50 Hz 命令控制。

items.capEn: Figure 5: Object Tracking Comparison. We compare Ours and FoundationPose [17] for object tracking with head-to-head human evaluations on 150 videos (left), and visualize samples (right).
items.capZh: 图 5：物体跟踪比较。我们在 150 个视频上通过头对头人类评估比较我们的方法与 FoundationPose [17] 的物体跟踪（左），并可视化样本（右）。

items.en: As shown in Fig. 5 (left), on the 150-video in-the-wild benchmark, human raters prefer our object tracking over the state-of-the-art FPose 67% of the time, with most videos receiving unanimous preferences. Qualitatively (Fig. 5), FPose loses the object under mild motion blur and occlusion, whereas our method recovers temporally consistent translations and rotations across the full clip. This advantage carries over to the standard hand-object reconstruction benchmarks in Table 2: we establish a new state-of-the-art on both DexYCB and HOI4D, outperforming all baselines.
items.zh: 如图 5（左）所示，在 150 个视频的野外基准上，人类评估者偏好我们的物体跟踪超过最先进的 FPose 的比例为 67%，大多数视频获得一致偏好。定性上（图 5），FPose 在轻微运动模糊和遮挡下丢失物体，而我们的方法在整个片段中恢复时间一致的平移和旋转。这一优势延续到表 2 的标准手-物体重建基准：我们在 DexYCB 和 HOI4D 上都建立了新的最先进水平，优于所有基线。

items.en: In addition, we ablate design choices for our object tracking in the Appendix. We see that adaptive pose guidance via point tracking consistently improves reconstruction quality, and clustering-based selection performs on par with pose-likelihood selection while being up to 30×\times faster.
items.zh: 此外，我们在附录中消融了物体跟踪的设计选择。我们看到通过点跟踪的自适应姿态引导持续改善重建质量，而基于聚类的选择与姿态似然选择性能相当，同时速度快达 30 倍。

items.en: We report retargeting results in Table 3. On our reconstructed in-the-wild data, Do as I Do reaches a 71% success rate, significantly improving over the baseline of 25%. The main differentiator is warmup, which discovers initial states that are much more stable and natural than the noisy initial frame, thereby leading to successful tracking in subsequent timesteps. Additionally, we find that perturbation noticeably improves the qualitative results (e.g., natural grasps) despite marginally affecting the quantitative metrics, and our transition reward encourages successful picks and places for trajectories that otherwise would’ve missed the object during crucial transition timesteps.
items.zh: 我们在表 3 中报告了重定向结果。在我们的重建野外数据上，“照我做”达到了 71% 的成功率，显著优于 25% 的基线。主要区别在于预热，它发现了比噪声初始帧更稳定和自然的初始状态，从而在后续时间步中实现成功跟踪。此外，我们发现扰动在定性结果上（例如，自然抓取）有显著改善，尽管对定量指标影响不大，而我们的过渡奖励鼓励了成功拾取和放置，否则轨迹会在关键过渡时间步中错过物体。

items.en: Further validating our method on OakInk2, we also see consistent improvement with the introduction of each component, moving from a baseline of 72% up to 81%. This shows that our retargeting, despite being designed for imperfect reconstructed references, produces effective gains even with clean MoCap trajectories, and scales well to the 1,000+ bimanual tasks in this benchmark.
items.zh: 进一步在 OakInk2 上验证我们的方法，我们也看到每个组件的引入带来了一致的改进，从 72% 的基线上升到 81%。这表明我们的重定向，尽管是为不完美的重建参考设计的，即使在干净的 MoCap 轨迹上也产生了有效的增益，并且在该基准的 1000 多个双手任务上扩展良好。

items.capEn: Figure 6: Real-World Deployment. We showcase trajectories for 10 tasks: whisking, pouring, dusting, squeezing, tamping, erasing, stirring, hammering, spreading, and picking.
items.capZh: 图 6：真实世界部署。我们展示了 10 个任务的轨迹：搅拌、倾倒、除尘、挤压、夯实、擦除、搅动、锤击、涂抹和拾取。

items.en: In total, our pipeline produced 500 high-quality, human-verified dexterous manipulation trajectories across internet (53%), egocentric (31%), and generated (16%) videos. To demonstrate quality, we execute a representative set of trajectories in the real world. We choose 10 motions with various object geometries and grasp classes [74], including writing tripod, power, ventral, and parallel extension grasps. After gravity alignment, the reconstructions still follow the videos’ camera coordinates, so we manually align the initial pose (x,y,zx,y,z, yaw) with the robot workspace in simulation before computing arm IK and deploying in the real world. Results are shown in Fig. 6, further film strips are presented in the Appendix, and videos are presented on our webpage.
items.zh: 总共，我们的流程产生了 500 条高质量、经过人工验证的灵巧操作轨迹，分别来自互联网（53%）、自我中心（31%）和生成（16%）视频。为了展示质量，我们在真实世界中执行了一组代表性轨迹。我们选择了 10 种具有不同物体几何形状和抓取类别 [74] 的动作，包括书写三脚架、强力、掌心和平行伸展抓取。在重力对齐后，重建仍遵循视频的相机坐标，因此我们在计算手臂逆运动学并在真实世界部署之前，手动将初始姿态（x, y, z, 偏航）与模拟中的机器人工作空间对齐。结果如图 6 所示，更多胶片条在附录中展示，视频在我们的网页上呈现。

items.en: Given recent interest in human data for scaling up robotics [30, 33, 75], this section aims to highlight common quality issues in online human data sources. We noticed these patterns while analyzing common datasets, and present an analysis performed on 100DOH [76]. However, these lessons should be applicable more generally. We start with 2,000 10-second clips sampled from 100DOH, which has already been filtered for hand-object interaction, and find that only 187 clips (9%) have meaningful hand-object interaction present. Out of these 187 candidate clips, 41 clips have the hand or object outside the video boundary, and 29 clips have no activity or activity that spans across shot boundaries. Further 14 clips fail due to camera motion, and another 10 clips fail because of SAM 3D, both of which may be fixed in the future with better models. We lose another 10 clips for other reasons. Out of the 2,000 videos sampled from 100DOH, only 83 (4%) survive our quality check for the reconstruction pass. Even in the best case, we foresee 107 clips, or roughly 5% of the data, being directly relevant for learning dexterous manipulation, implying a 20×20\times penalty in not properly preprocessing and filtering internet videos for robot learning.
items.zh: 鉴于近期对人类数据用于扩展机器人技术的兴趣 [30, 33, 75]，本节旨在强调在线人类数据源中的常见质量问题。我们在分析常见数据集时注意到了这些模式，并呈现了对 100DOH [76] 的分析。然而，这些经验应更普遍适用。我们从 100DOH 中采样了 2,000 个 10 秒片段，这些片段已经过手-物体交互过滤，发现只有 187 个片段（9%）存在有意义的手-物体交互。在这 187 个候选片段中，41 个片段的手或物体在视频边界外，29 个片段没有活动或活动跨越镜头边界。另外 14 个片段因相机运动失败，另有 10 个片段因 SAM 3D 失败，这两者未来可能通过更好的模型修复。我们又因其他原因丢失了 10 个片段。在从 100DOH 采样的 2,000 个视频中，只有 83 个（4%）通过了重建的质量检查。即使在最佳情况下，我们预计 107 个片段，即大约 5% 的数据，与学习灵巧操作直接相关，这意味着如果不适当预处理和过滤互联网视频用于机器人学习，将产生 20 倍的惩罚。

items.en: We introduced Do as I Do, a framework for reconstructing and retargeting everyday human videos onto dexterous robot hands. Our method is effective across egocentric, exocentric, and online video sources, showing a path towards scaling robot data by simply observing humans. We hope Do as I Do pushes us closer to making human videos first-class citizens in the robot learning data landscape.
items.zh: 我们介绍了“照我做”，一个将日常人类视频重建并重定向到灵巧机器人手的框架。我们的方法在自我中心、外部中心和在线视频源上均有效，展示了通过简单观察人类来扩展机器人数据的路径。我们希望“照我做”能推动我们更接近使人类视频成为机器人学习数据领域的一等公民。

items.en: Limitations. Our approach assumes rigid objects and semi-accurate metric depth predictions from monocular RGB, and may fail when either assumption doesn’t hold. Monocular observations also suffer from ambiguity in the true hand-object distance, making it difficult to distinguish physical contact from mere visual occlusion. In addition, our method reconstructs only the hand and an object, rather than the full scene. As a result, it cannot reason about environmental constraints such as obstacles or articulations. Such scene-level reasoning remains important even with perfectly accurate references, since human intention is expressed through not only hand-object but also hand-scene interactions. Finally, the current physics simulators model the real world dynamics only approximately, which places an upper bound on the achievable real-world performance of our framework.
items.zh: 局限性。我们的方法假设刚体物体和来自单目 RGB 的半准确度量深度预测，当任一假设不成立时可能失败。单目观测还受到真实手-物体距离模糊性的影响，难以区分物理接触与单纯视觉遮挡。此外，我们的方法仅重建手和一个物体，而非完整场景。因此，它无法推理环境约束，如障碍物或关节。即使有完全准确的参考，这种场景级推理仍然重要，因为人类意图不仅通过手-物体交互表达，也通过手-场景交互表达。最后，当前的物理模拟器仅近似模拟真实世界动力学，这为我们的框架在真实世界中的可实现性能设置了上限。

items.en: We thank Kyutai for providing us with the compute resources for this project. We are grateful to Chaoyi Pan for guidance and insightful discussions on retargeting. We also thank Jane Wu and Hongsuk Choi for helpful advice and discussions on hand-object reconstruction. This work was supported by ONR MURI N00014-21-1-280. Haritheja Etukuru and William Liang are supported by the NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program under Grant DGE 2146752. Pieter Abbeel holds concurrent appointments as a Professor at UC Berkeley and as an Amazon Scholar at Amazon. This paper describes work performed at UC Berkeley and is not associated with Amazon.
items.zh: 我们感谢 Kyutai 为本项目提供算力资源。感谢潘超逸在重定向方面给予的指导和富有洞见的讨论。同时感谢 Jane Wu 和 Choi Hongsuk 在手物重建方面提供的宝贵建议与讨论。本研究得到 ONR MURI N00014-21-1-280 的资助。Haritheja Etukuru 和 William Liang 受美国国家科学基金会研究生研究奖学金计划资助（Grant DGE 2146752）。Pieter Abbeel 同时担任加州大学伯克利分校教授和亚马逊学者。本文描述的工作在加州大学伯克利分校完成，与亚马逊无关。
